reason: All 5 complexity flags are false positives due to negation-insensitive keyword matching. The validator matches terms like 'authentication', 'real-time', 'database', etc. in contexts where the PRD explicitly says NO to these features.

flag_analysis.prd_text: Account System: None — single-user local app, no auth needed
flag_analysis.actual: NO AUTH. Single-user localStorage app. Shareable links not even needed.

flag_analysis.prd_text: No real-time or collaboration — Solo tool. No WebSockets, no shared state.
flag_analysis.actual: NO REAL-TIME. Pure client-side CRUD. No WebSockets, no server push.

flag_analysis.prd_text: Storage: localStorage with JSON export/import (no server database)
flag_analysis.actual: NO SERVER DATABASE. All data in browser localStorage. Export/import JSON for backup.

flag_analysis.prd_text: Toggle repurposing status (done/planned/not started), add notes per platform
flag_analysis.actual: Simple CRUD + toggle. No complex workflows, no automation, no multi-step processes.

flag_analysis.prd_text: Single-user local app. Your content, your device.
flag_analysis.actual: SOLO APP. No multi-user, no collaboration, no sharing.

p0_count_analysis.validator_reason: Matches 'P0', 'P1', 'P2' strings in feature definitions section
actual_p0_features: Content Detail View with Repurposing Notes
p0_count_analysis.verdict: Validator overcounts due to matching P1/P2 in the 'Post-MVP Features' section header text

final_assessment.rationale: 6-hour build, 3 P0 features, no auth, no database, no real-time, no APIs. Pure client-side CRUD app with localStorage. 2-hour buffer within 8-hour limit. Classic overnight build.
